heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimens for Chancroid
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose
or
Ceftriaxone 250 mg IM in a single dose
or
Ciprofloxacin 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 3 days
or
Erythromycin base 500 mg orally 3 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers
First Clinical Episode of Genital Herpes

headers: Recommended Regimens for First Clinical Episode of Genital Herpes*
rows: Acyclovir † 400 mg orally 3 times/day for 7–10 days
or
Famciclovir 250 mg orally 3 times/day for 7–10 days
or
Valacyclovir 1 g orally 2 times/day for 7–10 days
* Treatment can be extended if healing is incomplete after 10 days of therapy.
† Acyclovir 200 mg orally 5 times/day is also effective but is not recommended because of the frequency of dosing.

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers
Suppressive Therapy for Recurrent HSV-2 Genital Herpes

headers: Recommended Regimens for Suppression of Recurrent HSV-2 Genital Herpes
rows: Acyclovir 400 mg orally 2 times/day
or
Valacyclovir 500 mg orally once a day*
or
Valacyclovir 1 g orally once a day
or
Famciclovir 250 mg orally 2 times/day
* Valacyclovir 500 mg once a day might be less effective than other valacyclovir or acyclovir dosing regimens for persons who have frequent recurrences (i.e., ≥10 episodes/year).

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers
Episodic Therapy for Recurrent HSV-2 Genital Herpes

headers: Recommended Regimens for Episodic Therapy for Recurrent HSV-2 Genital Herpes*
rows: Acyclovir 800 mg orally 2 times/day for 5 days
or
Acyclovir 800 mg orally 3 times/day for 2 days
or
Famciclovir 1 g orally 2 times/day for 1 day
or
Famciclovir 500 mg orally once, followed by 250 mg 2 times/day for 2 days
or
Famciclovir 125 mg orally 2 times/day for 5 days
or
Valacyclovir 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 3 days
or
Valacyclovir 1 g orally once daily for 5 days
*Acyclovir 400 mg orally 3 times/day for 5 days is also effective but is not recommended because of frequency of dosing.

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimens for Daily Suppression of Genital Herpes Among Persons with HIV Infection
rows: Acyclovir 400–800 mg orally 2–3 times/day
or
Famciclovir 500 mg orally 2 times/day
or
Valacyclovir 500 mg orally 2 times/day

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimens for Episodic Genital Herpes Infection Among Persons with HIV Infection
rows: Acyclovir 400 mg orally 3 times/day for 5–10 days
or
Famciclovir 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 5–10 days
or
Valacyclovir 1 g orally 2 times/day for 5–10 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimen for Suppression of Recurrent Genital Herpes Among Pregnant Women*
rows: Acyclovir 400 mg orally 3 times/day
or
Valacyclovir 500 mg orally 2 times/day
* Treatment recommended starting at 36 weeks’ gestation.

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimen for Granuloma Inguinale (Donovanosis)
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally once weekly or 500 mg daily for >3 weeks and until all lesions have completely healed

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for at least 3 weeks and until all lesions have completely healed
or
Erythromycin base 500 mg orally 4 times/day for >3 weeks and until all lesions have completely healed
or
Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole one double-strength (160 mg/800 mg) tablet orally 2 times/day for >3 weeks and until all lesions have completely healed

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Recommended Regimen for Lymphogranuloma Venereum
rows: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 21 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Genital, Anal, or Perianal Ulcers

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally once weekly for 3 weeks*
or
Erythromycin base 500 mg orally 4 times/day for 21 days
* Because this regimen has not been validated, a test of cure with C. trachomatis NAAT 4 weeks after completion of treatment can be considered.

headers: Recommended Regimen for Primary and Secondary Syphilis* Among Adults
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM in a single dose

* Recommendations for treating syphilis among persons with HIV infection and pregnant women are discussed elsewhere in this report (see Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection; Syphilis During Pregnancy).

headers: Recommended Regimen for Syphilis Among Infants and Children
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight IM, up to the adult dose of 2.4 million units in a single dose

headers: Recommended Regimens for Latent Syphilis* Among Adults
rows: Early latent syphilis: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM in a single dose
Late latent syphilis: Benzathine penicillin G 7.2 million units total, administered as 3 doses of 2.4 million units IM each at 1-week intervals
* Recommendations for treating syphilis in persons with HIV and pregnant women are discussed elsewhere in this report (see Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection; Syphilis During Pregnancy).

headers: Recommended Regimen for Tertiary Syphilis Among Adults
rows: Tertiary syphilis with normal CSF examination: Benzathine penicillin G 7.2 million units total, administered as 3 doses of 2.4 million units IM each at 1-week intervals

headers: Recommended Regimen for Neurosyphilis, Ocular Syphilis, or Otosyphilis Among Adults
rows: Aqueous crystalline penicillin G 18–24 million units per day, administered as 3–4 million units IV every 4 hours or continuous infusion for 10–14 days

headers: Alternative Regimen
rows: Procaine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM once daily
plus
Probenecid 500 mg orally 4 times/day, both for 10–14 days

heading_path: Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection
Primary and Secondary Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection

headers: Recommended Regimen for Primary and Secondary Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM in a single dose

heading_path: Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection
Latent Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection

headers: Recommended Regimen for Early Latent Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 2.4 million units IM in a single dose

heading_path: Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection
Latent Syphilis Among Persons with HIV Infection

headers: Recommended Regimen for Late Latent Syphilis or Latent Syphilis of Unknown Duration Among Persons with HIV Infection
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 7.2 million units total, administered as 3 doses of 2.4 million units IM at 1-week intervals

headers: Recommended Regimen for Syphilis During Pregnancy
rows: Pregnant women should be treated with the recommended penicillin regimen for their stage of infection

heading_path: Scenario 1: Confirmed Proven or Highly Probable Congenital Syphilis

headers: Recommended Regimens, Confirmed or Highly Probable Congenital Syphilis
rows: Aqueous crystalline penicillin G 100,000–150,000 units/kg body weight/day, administered as 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IV every 12 hours during the first 7 days of life and every 8 hours thereafter for a total of 10 days
or
Procaine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IM in a single daily dose for 10 days

headers: Recommended Regimens, Possible Congenital Syphilis
rows: Aqueous crystalline penicillin G 100,000–150,000 units/kg body weight/day, administered as 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IV every 12 hours during the first 7 days of life and every 8 hours thereafter for a total of 10 days
or
Procaine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IM in a single daily dose for 10 days
or
Benzathine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IM in a single dose

headers: Recommended Regimen, Congenital Syphilis Less Likely
rows: Benzathine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight/dose IM in a single dose*

* Another approach involves not treating the newborn if follow-up is certain but providing close serologic follow-up every 2–3 months for 6 months for infants whose mothers’ nontreponemal titers decreased at least fourfold after therapy for early syphilis or remained stable for low-titer, latent syphilis (e.g., VDRL <1:2 or RPR <1:4).

headers: Recommended Regimen, Congenital Syphilis Unlikely
rows: No treatment is required. However, any neonate with reactive nontreponemal tests should be followed serologically to ensure the nontreponemal test returns to negative (see Follow-Up). Benzathine penicillin G 50,000 units/kg body weight as a single IM injection might be considered, particularly if follow-up is uncertain and the neonate has a reactive nontreponemal test.

heading_path: Evaluation and Treatment of Infants and Children with Congenital Syphilis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Congenital Syphilis Among Infants and Children
rows: Aqueous crystalline penicillin G 200,000–300,000 units/kg body weight/day IV, administered as 50,000 units/kg body weight every 4–6 hours for 10 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Urethritis and Cervicitis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Nongonococcal Urethritis
rows: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Urethritis and Cervicitis

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose
or
Azithromycin 500 mg orally in a single dose; then 250 mg orally daily for 4 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Urethritis and Cervicitis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Cervicitis*
rows: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days
* Consider concurrent treatment for gonococcal infection if the patient is at risk for gonorrhea or lives in a community where the prevalence of gonorrhea is high (see Gonococcal Infections).

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Urethritis and Cervicitis

headers: Alternative Regimen
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose

heading_path: Chlamydial Infection Among Adolescents and Adults

headers: Recommended Regimen for Chlamydial Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
rows: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Chlamydial Infection Among Adolescents and Adults

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose
or
Levofloxacin 500 mg orally once daily for 7 days

heading_path: Chlamydial Infection Among Adolescents and Adults

headers: Recommended Regimen for Chlamydial Infection During Pregnancy
rows: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose

heading_path: Chlamydial Infection Among Adolescents and Adults

headers: Alternative Regimen
rows: Amoxicillin 500 mg orally 3 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Ophthalmia Neonatorum Caused by C. trachomatis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Chlamydial Infection Among Neonates
rows: Erythromycin base or ethyl succinate 50 mg/kg body weight/day orally, divided into 4 doses daily for 14 days*

* An association between oral erythromycin and azithromycin and infantile hypertrophic pyloric stenosis (IHPS) has been reported among infants aged <6 weeks. Infants treated with either of these antimicrobials should be followed for IHPS signs and symptoms.

heading_path: Infant Pneumonia Caused by C. trachomatis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Chlamydial Pneumonia Among Infants
rows: Erythromycin base or ethyl succinate 50 mg/kg body weight/day orally divided into 4 doses daily for 14 days

heading_path: Infant Pneumonia Caused by C. trachomatis

headers: Alternative Regimen
rows: Azithromycin suspension 20 mg/kg body weight/day orally, 1 dose daily for 3 days

heading_path: Chlamydial Infection Among Infants and Children

headers: Recommended Regimens for Chlamydial Infection Among Infants and Children
rows: For infants and children weighing <45 kg: Erythromycin base or ethyl succinate 50 mg/kg body weight/day orally divided into 4 doses daily for 14 days
Data are limited regarding the effectiveness and optimal dose of azithromycin for treating chlamydial infection among infants and children weighing <45 kg.
For children weighing ≥45 kg but aged <8 years: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose
For children aged ≥8 years: Azithromycin 1 g orally in a single dose
or
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Uncomplicated Gonococcal Infection of the Cervix, Urethra, or Rectum

headers: Recommended Regimen for Uncomplicated Gonococcal Infection of the Cervix, Urethra, or Rectum Among Adults and Adolescents
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose for persons weighing <150 kg
If chlamydial infection has not been excluded, treat for chlamydia with doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days.
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g ceftriaxone should be administered.

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Uncomplicated Gonococcal Infection of the Cervix, Urethra, or Rectum

headers: start highlight Alternative Regimens if Ceftriaxone Is Not Available end highlight
rows: start highlight Gentamicin 240 mg IM in a single dose end highlight
start highlight plus end highlight
start highlight Azithromycin 2 g orally in a single dose end highlight
start highlight or end highlight
start highlight Cefixime* 800 mg orally in a single dose end highlight
start highlight * If chlamydial infection has not been excluded, providers should treat for chlamydia with doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days. end highlight

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Uncomplicated Gonococcal Infection of the Pharynx

headers: Recommended Regimen for Uncomplicated Gonococcal Infection of the Pharynx Among Adolescents and Adults
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose for persons weighing <150 kg
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g ceftriaxone should be administered.

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults

headers: Recommended Regimen for Gonococcal Conjunctivitis Among Adolescents and Adults
rows: Ceftriaxone 1 g IM in a single dose
Providers should consider one-time lavage of the infected eye with saline solution.

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Treatment of Arthritis and Arthritis-Dermatitis Syndrome

headers: Recommended Regimen for Gonococcal-Related Arthritis and Arthritis-Dermatitis Syndrome
rows: Ceftriaxone 1 g IM or IV every 24 hours
If chlamydial infection has not been excluded, providers should treat for chlamydia with doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days.

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Treatment of Arthritis and Arthritis-Dermatitis Syndrome

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Cefotaxime 1 g IV every 8 hours
or
Ceftizoxime 1 g every 8 hours
If chlamydial infection has not been excluded, providers should treat for chlamydia with doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days.

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Adolescents and Adults
Treatment of Gonococcal Meningitis and Endocarditis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Gonococcal Meningitis and Endocarditis
rows: Ceftriaxone 1–2 g IV start highlight every 24 hours end highlight
If chlamydial infection has not been excluded, providers should treat for chlamydia with doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days.

heading_path: Preventing Ophthalmia Neonatorum Caused by N. gonorrhoeae

headers: Recommended Regimen to Prevent Ophthalmia Neonatorum Caused by N. gonorrhoeae
rows: Erythromycin 0.5% ophthalmic ointment in each eye in a single application at birth

heading_path: Preventing Ophthalmia Neonatorum Caused by N. gonorrhoeae

headers: Recommended Regimen for Gonococcal Ophthalmia Neonatorum
rows: Ceftriaxone 25–50 mg/kg body weight IV or IM in a single dose, not to exceed 250 mg

heading_path: Disseminated Gonococcal Infection and Gonococcal Scalp Abscesses Among Neonates

headers: Recommended Regimens for Disseminated Gonococcal Infection Among Neonates
rows: Ceftriaxone 25–50 mg/kg body weight/day IV or IM in a single daily dose for 7 days, with a duration of 10–14 days if meningitis is documented
or
Cefotaxime 25 mg/kg body weight/day IV or IM every 12 hours for 7 days, with a duration of 10–14 days if meningitis is documented

heading_path: Neonates Born to Mothers Who Have Gonococcal Infection
Treatment in the Absence of Signs of Gonococcal Infection

headers: Recommended Regimen for Neonates Without Signs of Gonococcal Infection
rows: Ceftriaxone start highlight 20–50 mg/kg end highlight body weight IV or IM in a single dose, not to exceed 250 mg

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Infants and Children

headers: Recommended Regimen for Uncomplicated Gonococcal Vulvovaginitis, Cervicitis, Urethritis, Pharyngitis, or Proctitis Among Infants and Children Weighing ≤45 kg
rows: Ceftriaxone 25–50 mg/kg body weight IV or IM in a single dose, not to exceed 250 mg IM

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Infants and Children

headers: Recommended Regimen for Uncomplicated Gonococcal Vulvovaginitis, Cervicitis, Urethritis, Pharyngitis, or Proctitis Among Children Weighing >45 kg
rows: Treat with the regimen recommended for adults (see Gonococcal Infections)

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Infants and Children

headers: Recommended Regimen for Bacteremia or Arthritis Among Children Weighing ≤45 kg
rows: Ceftriaxone 50 mg/kg body weight (maximum dose: 2 g) IM or IV in a single dose daily every 24 hours for 7 days

heading_path: Gonococcal Infection Among Infants and Children

headers: Recommended Regimen for Bacteremia or Arthritis Among Children Weighing >45 kg
rows: Ceftriaxone 1 g IM or IV in a single dose daily every 24 hours for 7 days

headers: Recommended Regimens if M. genitalium Resistance Testing Is Available
rows: If macrolide sensitive: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days, followed by azithromycin 1 g orally initial dose, followed by 500 mg orally once daily for 3 additional days (2.5 g total)
If macrolide resistant: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days followed by moxifloxacin 400 mg orally once daily for 7 days

headers: Recommended Regimen if M. genitalium Resistance Testing Is Not Available
rows: If M. genitalium is detected by an FDA-cleared NAAT: Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days, followed by moxifloxacin 400 mg orally once daily for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Recommended Regimens for Bacterial Vaginosis
rows: Metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days
or
Metronidazole gel 0.75% one full applicator (5 g) intravaginally, once daily for 5 days
or
Clindamycin cream 2% one full applicator (5 g) intravaginally at bedtime for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Clindamycin 300 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days
or
Clindamycin ovules 100 mg intravaginally once at bedtime for 3 days*
or
Secnidazole 2 g oral granules in a single dose †
or
Tinidazole 2 g orally once daily for 2 days
or
Tinidazole 1 g orally once daily for 5 days

* Clindamycin ovules use an oleaginous base that might weaken latex or rubber products (e.g., condoms and diaphragms). Use of such products within 72 hours after treatment with clindamycin ovules is not recommended.
† Oral granules should be sprinkled onto unsweetened applesauce, yogurt, or pudding before ingestion. A glass of water can be taken after administration to aid in swallowing.

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Recommended Regimen for Trichomoniasis Among Women
rows: Metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Recommended Regimen for Trichomoniasis Among Men
rows: Metronidazole 2 g orally in a single dose

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Alternative Regimen for Women and Men
rows: Tinidazole 2 g orally in a single dose

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Recommended Regimen for Trichomonas and HIV Infection Among Women
rows: Metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days

heading_path: Diseases Characterized by Vulvovaginal Itching, Burning, Irritation, Odor, or Discharge

headers: Recommended Regimens for Vulvovaginal Candidiasis
rows: Over-the-Counter Intravaginal Agents
Clotrimazole 1% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 7–14 days
or
Clotrimazole 2% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 3 days
or
Miconazole 2% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 7 days
or
Miconazole 4% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 3 days
or
Miconazole 100 mg vaginal suppository one suppository daily for 7 days
or
Miconazole 200 mg vaginal suppository one suppository for 3 days
or
Miconazole 1,200 mg vaginal suppository one suppository for 1 day
or
Tioconazole 6.5% ointment 5 g intravaginally in a single application
Prescription Intravaginal Agents
Butoconazole 2% cream (single-dose bioadhesive product) 5 g intravaginally in a single application
or
Terconazole 0.4% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 7 days
or
Terconazole 0.8% cream 5 g intravaginally daily for 3 days
or
Terconazole 80 mg vaginal suppository one suppository daily for 3 days
Oral Agent
Fluconazole 150 mg orally in a single dose

headers: Recommended Parenteral Regimens for Pelvic Inflammatory Disease
rows: Ceftriaxone 1 g IV every 24 hours
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally or IV every 12 hours
plus
Metronidazole 500 mg orally or IV every 12 hours
or
Cefotetan 2 g IV every 12 hours
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally or IV every 12 hours
or
Cefoxitin 2 g IV every 6 hours
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally or IV every 12 hours

headers: Alternative Parenteral Regimens
rows: Ampicillin-sulbactam 3 g IV every 6 hours
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally or IV every 12 hours
or
Clindamycin 900 mg IV every 8 hours
plus
Gentamicin loading dose IV or IM (2 mg/kg body weight), followed by a maintenance dose (1.5 mg/kg body weight) every 8 hours; single daily dosing (3–5 mg/kg body weight) can be substituted

headers: Recommended Intramuscular or Oral Regimens for Pelvic Inflammatory Disease
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days with metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days
or
Cefoxitin 2 g IM in a single dose and probenecid 1 g orally administered concurrently in a single dose
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days with metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days
or
Other parenteral third-generation cephalosporin (e.g., ceftizoxime or cefotaxime)
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days with metronidazole 500 mg orally 2 times/day for 14 days
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g of ceftriaxone should be administered.

headers: Recommended Regimens for Epididymitis
rows: For acute epididymitis most likely caused by chlamydia or gonorrhea: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 10 days
For acute epididymitis most likely caused by chlamydia, gonorrhea, or enteric organisms (men who practice insertive anal sex): Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose
plus
Levofloxacin 500 mg orally once daily for 10 days
For acute epididymitis most likely caused by enteric organisms only: Levofloxacin 500 mg orally once daily for 10 days
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g of ceftriaxone should be administered.

headers: Recommended Regimens for External Anogenital Warts (i.e., Penis, Groin, Scrotum, Vulva, Perineum, External Anus, or Perianus)*
rows: Patient-applied: Imiquimod 3.75% or 5% cream †
or
Podofilox 0.5% solution or gel
or
Sinecatechins 15% ointment †
Provider-administered: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen or cryoprobe
or
Surgical removal by tangential scissor excision, tangential shave excision, curettage, laser, or electrosurgery
or
Trichloroacetic acid (TCA) or bichloroacetic acid (BCA) 80%–90% solution

* Persons with external anal or perianal warts might also have intra-anal warts. Thus, persons with external anal warts might benefit from an inspection of the anal canal by digital examination, standard anoscopy, or high-resolution anoscopy.
† Might weaken condoms and vaginal diaphragms.

heading_path: Alternative Regimens for External Genital Warts

headers: Recommended Regimens for Urethral Meatus Warts
rows: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen
or
Surgical removal

heading_path: Alternative Regimens for External Genital Warts

headers: Recommended Regimens for Vaginal Warts
rows: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen
The use of a cryoprobe in the vagina is not recommended because of the risk for vaginal perforation and fistula formation.
or
Surgical removal
or
Trichloracetic acid (TCA) or bichloroacetic acid (BCA) 80%–90% solution

heading_path: Alternative Regimens for External Genital Warts

headers: Recommended Regimens for Cervical Warts
rows: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen
or
Surgical removal
or
Trichloracetic acid (TCA) or bichloroacetic acid (BCA) 80%–90% solution

Management of cervical warts should include consultation with a specialist. For women who have exophytic cervical warts, a biopsy evaluation to exclude HSIL should be performed before treatment is initiated.

heading_path: Alternative Regimens for External Genital Warts

headers: Recommended Regimens for Intra-Anal Warts
rows: Cryotherapy with liquid nitrogen
or
Surgical removal
or
Trichloracetic acid (TCA) or bichloroacetic acid (BCA) 80%–90% solution
Management of intra-anal warts should include consultation with a colorectal specialist.

heading_path: Diagnostic and Treatment Considerations for Acute Proctitis

headers: Recommended Regimen for Acute Proctitis
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose<
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 7 days †
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g of ceftriaxone should be administered.
† Doxycycline course should be extended to 100 mg orally 2 times/day for 21 days in the presence of bloody discharge, perianal or mucosal ulcers, or tenesmus and a positive rectal chlamydia test.

headers: Recommended Regimens for Pediculosis Pubis
rows: Permethrin 1% cream rinse applied to affected areas and washed off after 10 minutes
or
Pyrethrin with piperonyl butoxide applied to the affected area and washed off after 10 minutes

headers: Alternative Regimens
rows: Malathion 0.5% lotion applied to affected areas and washed off after 8–12 hours
or
Ivermectin 250 µ g/kg body weight orally, repeated in 7–14 days

headers: Recommended Regimens for Scabies
rows: Permethrin 5% cream applied to all areas of the body from the neck down and washed off after 8–14 hours
or
Ivermectin 200 ug/kg body weight orally, repeated in 14 days*
start highlight or end highlight
start highlight Ivermectin 1% lotion applied to all areas of the body from the neck down and washed off after 8–14 hours; repeat treatment in 1 week if symptoms persist end highlight
* Oral ivermectin has limited ovicidal activity; a second dose is required for eradication.

headers: Alternative Regimen
rows: Lindane 1% 1 oz of lotion or 30 g of cream applied in a thin layer to all areas of the body from the neck down and thoroughly washed off after 8 hours*
* Infants and children aged <10 years should not be treated with lindane.

heading_path: Sexual Assault and Abuse and STIs

headers: Recommended Regimen for Adolescent and Adult Female Sexual Assault Survivors
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg 2 times/day orally for 7 days
plus
Metronidazole 500 mg 2 times/day orally for 7 days
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g of ceftriaxone should be administered.

heading_path: Sexual Assault and Abuse and STIs

headers: Recommended Regimen for Adolescent and Adult Male Sexual Assault Survivors
rows: Ceftriaxone 500 mg* IM in a single dose
plus
Doxycycline 100 mg 2 times/day orally for 7 days
* For persons weighing ≥150 kg, 1 g of ceftriaxone should be administered.
